title: Migrate Assistant/Audit/Security legacy v0.2 CSS classes to paper aesthetic

findings.description: While Dashboard, Explorer, Intelligence, Settings, Import, Jobs, Maintenance, Integrations, Onboarding, Lock, and Schedule all use the paper outer wrapper convention (mx-auto max-w-[1080px] flex flex-col pt-7 or PaperCard), the Assistant page at lines 323, 340, 360, and 419 still wraps all content in <section className='page-shell'> and uses .panel / .panel-header / .panel-body / .panel-action / .dashboard-next-action / .result-row / .intelligence-job-list CSS classes. The Audit page similarly uses page-shell (lines 376, 384, 395, 412, 434) with .panel class patterns. These are v0.2 CSS constructs that produce a visibly different visual rhythm -- no paper card borders, different padding, different background -- creating 'style islands' in the navigation flow.
findings.suggestedFix: For Audit: migrate from page-shell + panel classes to the paper outer wrapper + PaperCard composition that the other Operations routes use (Import, Jobs). For Assistant: since the AI features are v0.3-deferred and the current disabled state still displays, at minimum wrap the deferred-state callout in a PaperCard and use the paper outer wrapper container. The full active-AI conversation surface can wait for v0.3. Track in BACKLOG if not addressed this milestone.
